definition: Knowledge cutoff is the approximate end date of the training data for a given Claude model snapshot; events, products, and facts after that date are not reliably known unless you provide them in the prompt or via tools/retrieval.
layman: Claude's built-in memory of the world stops around when that model version was trained—so it won't know today's news or last week's product launch unless you tell it or hook up search.
content:
## Knowledge cutoff

Every released model carries a **knowledge cutoff** tied to pretraining (and sometimes partial updates documented by Anthropic). It is **not** a live feed of the internet.

### What cutoff means in practice

| You ask | Without tools/context |
| --- | --- |
| "Who won yesterday's game?" | Unreliable or refused |
| "Summarize this pasted article" | Works if text is in the prompt |
| "Our internal project codename?" | Unknown unless you provide it |
| "Current Claude model pricing" | May be stale—check docs |

Always verify **time-sensitive** facts on official sources.

### Cutoff vs context vs memory

| Mechanism | What it provides |
| --- | --- |
| **Training cutoff** | Statistical knowledge frozen near a date |
| **Prompt context** | Facts you paste this request |
| **Tools / web search** | Fresh external data when enabled |
| **Product memory** | User-controlled notes where feature exists |

They are **different channels**—cutoff does not disappear because you chatted yesterday.

### Model versions change cutoffs

Newer **model IDs** may have later cutoffs. When upgrading models in production:

- Re-run evals on time-sensitive prompts.
- Update user-facing disclaimers.
- Do not assume backward compatibility of factual answers.

Find cutoff notes in **Anthropic model documentation** for each snapshot. **As of June 2026**, exact cutoff dates vary by model ID—confirm on [docs.anthropic.com](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models) rather than relying on this page alone.

### Mitigation patterns

1. **Paste sources** — articles, release notes, JSON exports.
2. **Enable retrieval/tools** — API web search or your RAG pipeline.
3. **Ask for uncertainty** — "If unsure after cutoff, say unknown."
4. **Date-stamp requirements** — "Use only information after 2024-01-01 from the attached doc."

### Relationship to hallucination

Past cutoff, Claude may **confabulate** recent events instead of saying "I don't know." Combine cutoff awareness with **verification** habits from the Limitations topic.
keyPoints: Cutoff is the approximate end of training data for a model version.
Recent, private, and internal facts need context or tools.
Newer model releases may update cutoff—check docs per model ID.
Cutoff differs from chat memory and from pasted prompt context.
Ask for uncertainty and verify time-sensitive claims externally.
